'''Sam Hall''' (born {{birth date|2002|05|08|df=y}}) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} or {{rlp|lf}} for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously spent time on loan and dual registration from Castleford at the [[Midlands Hurricanes]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]], and at the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

== Background ==
Hall was born in [[Leeds]], West Yorkshire, England.

He studied at [[Garforth Academy]].

Hall played junior rugby league for [[Oulton Raiders]]. He joined the [[Castleford Tigers]] development system aged 14.

== Playing career ==

===Castleford Tigers===
In February 2020, Hall had been preparing to play for Castleford's reserves, but injuries to teammates saw him drafted into the first team aged 17.<ref>{{Cite web |date=10 February 2020 |title=Young Tigers reflect on Wigan win |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=6794 |access-date=14 November 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> He made his [[Super League]] debut for the [[Castleford Tigers]] ([[List of Castleford Tigers players|Heritage No. 998]]) against the [[Wigan Warriors]] on 7 February 2020.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2020-02-07 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 16-12 Wigan Warriors - Olpherts sets up win with first Tigers try |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/51359805 |access-date=2022-04-05 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> Weeks later, he signed full-time with the Tigers on a 2-year contract.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2020-02-26 |title=Sam Hall signs full-time with Tigers |url=https://www.castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=6820 |access-date=2022-04-05 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref>

Ahead of the 2021 season, Hall aimed for further Super League experience, and said, "it's a hard, hard challenge to break through with the talent of the older players, but if I get a chance I've just got to take it and play as well as I can."<ref>{{Cite web |date=10 March 2021 |title=Hall has high hopes for 2021 |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=7287 |access-date=14 November 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> Hall made three further first-team appearances for Castleford that year, as well as standout performances for the under-19s.<ref name="CT extension 2021">{{Cite web |date=2021-10-02 |title=Sam Hall extends for 2022 |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=7653 |access-date=2022-04-05 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> He signed a one-year contract extension in October 2021.<ref name="CT extension 2021" />

In June 2022, Hall signed a two-year contract extension at Castleford, keeping him with the Tigers until the end of 2024.<ref>{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=8 June 2022 |title=Castleford youngster Sam Hall signs new deal with Tigers |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-youngster-sam-hall-signs-new-deal-with-tigers |access-date=14 November 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He said, "I feel very much at home, I like the environment and the players. There was never any doubt - I wanted to stay here."<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=8 June 2022 |title=Castleford Tigers tie down academy product as club continue to plan for 2023 |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-tie-down-academy-product-as-club-continue-to-plan-for-2023-3724989 |access-date=14 November 2023 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> Head coach [[Lee Radford]] added, "Sam has a smart football IQ and a great skill set. He plays in a position where there is a genuine pathway for him at the club."<ref>{{Cite web |date=8 June 2022 |title=Sam Hall signs extension |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8106 |access-date=14 November 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref>

The 2023 season was a breakout personal year for Hall, as he established himself a regular place in Castleford's first-team from April. He scored his first try for the Tigers on 2 September against the [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2 September 2023 |title=Super League: Warrington Wolves 66-12 Castleford Tigers - Wire demolish struggling Tigers |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66661853 |access-date=14 November 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He made a total of 16 appearances as a {{Rlp|pr}} or {{Rlp|lf}} as the club narrowly survived a relegation battle with [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]].<ref name="RLRKC" /><ref>{{cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=22 September 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers ace Alex Mellor delivers brutally honest assessment of 'awful' season; 'We've massively underperformed' |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-ace-alex-mellor-delivers-brutally-honest-assessment-of-awful-season-weve-massively/ |access-date=14 November 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

On 5 April 2024, Hall scored his second [[Super League]] try for Castleford against the [[Salford Red Devils]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=5 April 2024 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 36-24 Salford Red Devils - Cas get first win of season |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68723225 |access-date=6 April 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

==== Midlands Hurricanes (dual registration) ====
Hall made one appearance for the [[Midlands Hurricanes]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]] during the 2022 season, through their dual registration agreement with Castleford. He returned to the club in the following year, making one further appearance for the Hurricanes in 2023.<ref name="RLRKC" />

==== London Broncos (loan) ====
In June 2022, Hall joined the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] on loan.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Ibbetson |first=Stephen |date=10 June 2022 |title=Castleford Tigers prospect heads to London Broncos on loan |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-prospect-heads-to-london-broncos-on-loan/ |access-date=14 November 2023 |website=TotalRL}}</ref> Head coach [[Mike Eccles]] said, "Sam is a big unit and a true ball-playing middle who has fantastic ability as both a carrier and being able to shift possession around the field."<ref>{{Cite web |date=10 June 2022 |title=Hall heads to London on month loan |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8111 |access-date=14 November 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> He made six appearances at {{Rlp|pr}} or {{Rlp|lf}} for the Broncos and scored tries against [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]], [[Sheffield Eagles|Sheffield]] and [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]].<ref name="RLRKC" />
